steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on both sides of the blank
steps.content: The left side — "In many languages, including English, these consonants are rare" — is a complete independent clause. The right side — "in some dialects of Mandarin, however, four such consonants are relatively common" — is also a complete independent clause. The blank must join them correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — rare,: A comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice, which is a grammatical error. ✗
  • Choice B — rare: No punctuation between two independent clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — rare;: A semicolon correctly joins two closely related independent clauses — precisely the rule that applies here. ✓
  • Choice D — rare and: The coordinating conjunction "and" without a comma before it creates an ungrammatical structure when connecting two independent clauses of this length and form. ✗
That matches Choice C — rare;.
